source: UCPMP 2024 - Government of India, Dept of Pharmaceuticals
rule_text: All promotional claims must be accurate, balanced, fair, objective and unambiguous and must be based on current evidence.

source: UCPMP 2024 - Government of India, Dept of Pharmaceuticals
rule_text: Claims about side effects must be presented in a fair and balanced manner, and should not minimize or omit relevant safety information.

source: UCPMP 2024 - Government of India, Dept of Pharmaceuticals
rule_text: The word 'safe' must not be used without qualification. No drug is universally safe. Claims of safety must be accompanied by context and evidence of the specific population studied.

source: UCPMP 2024 - Government of India, Dept of Pharmaceuticals
rule_text: Comparative claims against competitor products are prohibited unless supported by head-to-head clinical trial data published in a peer-reviewed journal.

source: UCPMP 2024 - Government of India, Dept of Pharmaceuticals
rule_text: No gifts, pecuniary advantages or benefits in kind may be offered or given to healthcare professionals. This includes travel, hospitality, cash, and any items of monetary value.

source: UCPMP 2024 - Government of India, Dept of Pharmaceuticals
rule_text: Free samples of drugs may only be provided to healthcare professionals who are qualified to prescribe such drugs, and must be marked 'Physician Sample - Not for Sale'.

source: UCPMP 2024 - Government of India, Dept of Pharmaceuticals
rule_text: All promotional material must include: the brand name, generic name (INN), name of each active ingredient, approved indications, dosage and administration, side effects, contraindications, warnings, and the name and address of the manufacturer.

source: UCPMP 2024 - Government of India, Dept of Pharmaceuticals
rule_text: Promotional claims must not use the term 'new' for any product that has been available in the market for more than 12 months.
